---
title: "Nish Etige"
subtitle: "Nishchitha Sandeepana Silva Etige (he/him)"
image: assets/images/headshot2.jpg
about: 
  template: trestles
  image-shape: rounded
  image-width: 15em
  links:
    - text: ResearchGate
      href: https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Etige-Nishchitha-Sandeepana-Silva-2
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/nishchitha-etige-22713425/
    - icon: google
      text: Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=0IW8468AAAAJ&hl=en
    - icon: twitter
      text: Twitter
      href: https://twitter.com/nishetige
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/nishsilva
    - icon: mail
      text: Email
      href: mailto:nishetige@gmail.com
---

# **Hello there, Welcome!**

I am a Climate Data Scientist specialized in developing data pipelines, analyzing large-scale ocean-atmosphere interactions, and building statistical and machine learning models on large spatiotemporal geophysical datasets.

I have expereince in industry and academia  where I utiilized numerical climate models, reanalysis, and remote sensing products to quantify the impacts of extreme weather and climate variability on physical and human systems.

Currently, I am exploring the potential of using AI and LLMs to improve the geospatial data exploration and analytical approcahes.\

### **Recent Highlights**

**I'm currently open to exciting opportunities in climate and data science, AI applications, and geospatial data solutions.**

-   Dive into my [**AI-powered Gulf Stream WCR Explorer**](https://wcr-ai.streamlit.app/)**.**

-   Uncover insights from our latest [**Machine Learning Review Paper**](https://doi.org/10.1007/s44295-025-00076-1)**.**

-   Our [**Science Advances**](https://doi.org/10.1126/sciadv.adw3661) article on low-frequency sea-level modes.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Education

**PhD in Earth & Environment** (2024)

Boston University Graduate School of Arts & Sciences

**MS in Marine Science & Technology** (2019)

University of Massachusetts Dartmouth's School for Marine Science & Technology \| New Bedford, MA

**BS in Aquatic Resources Technology** (2015)

Uva Wellassa University \| Sri Lanka
